stem: The equation N(t) = 8(1.6)t gives the estimated number of employees at a startup, where t is the number of years since the startup was founded. Which of the following is the best interpretation of the number 8 in this context?

answerOptions.content: The increase in the estimated number of employees each year
The percent increase in the estimated number of employees each year
The estimated number of employees when the startup was founded
The number of years the startup has been in operation

rationale: Choice C is correct. For an exponential function of the form N(t) = a(b)t, the initial value N(0) equals a. Since N(0) = 8(1.6)0 = 8, the number 8 represents the estimated number of employees when the startup was founded (t = 0). Choice A is wrong because the yearly increase is not constant for an exponential model. Choice B confuses 8 with the growth factor 1.6. Choice D describes t, not the constant 8.
